summary: Iter10 Sprint 5 — Audit log infra + Slack/Discord alerts + per-app cron + Managed Databases + PR Preview Deploys. 29/31 GREEN (93.5%). Two failures both stem from one real backend bug: send_alert() in services/notifications_sms.py only iterates ('sms','whatsapp','email') when building its available-channels list, so slack/discord channels are filtered out BEFORE the slack/discord dispatcher branch is reached. The spec calls for status:'skipped' when channel='slack'/'discord' and no webhook URL is configured, but the endpoint returns {results:[]} instead. All other Sprint 5 contracts pass cleanly. Verified: (1) Audit infra — auth.login writes workspace_id=null + actor_email; app.create writes workspace_id, resource_type='app', resource_id, meta.{name,repo_url}; app.delete writes a matching row. Fire-and-forget grace was 300ms. (2) GET /api/audit-log returns {entries[], limit} sorted DESC by created_at; action filter narrows results; cursor pagination via `before` returns only entries with created_at < cursor; non-member workspace_id → 403; missing workspace_id for non-admin → 403, admin → 200. (3) GET /api/audit-log/actions returns alphabetically-sorted distinct action names. (4) Notification prefs — supported_channels=['sms','whatsapp','email','slack','discord'], slack/discord_webhook_url returned; PUT rejects slack URLs not starting with https://hooks.slack.com/ (400) and discord URLs not starting with https://discord.com/api/webhooks/ (400); empty payload accepted. (5) POST /api/notifications/test with channel='push' → 400 (✓), but channel='slack'/'discord' with no URL → returns {results:[]} instead of skipped (✗ — see backend bug). (6) Cron CRUD — invalid 5-field expression rejected (400); create returns the persisted row with coolify_task_uuid=null (Coolify unreachable); list/update/delete all work; supports_coolify_sync flag mirrors app.coolify_app_uuid presence; cron.create + cron.delete audit rows confirmed. (7) Databases — GET returns supported_types with exactly {postgresql, redis, mysql, mariadb, mongodb}; POST {type='postgresql', name='TEST_pg', version='16'} creates with status='provisioning', connection_string_set=false; POST /reveal returns {connection_string:null, reason:'not provisioned yet'}; invalid type 'oracle' → 400; /start sets running, /stop sets stopped, DELETE removes + writes database.delete audit. (8) PR Previews — GET initial list returns {previews:[], parent_app_id}; PR opened (HMAC-signed) creates a child app in db.apps with is_pr_preview=true, parent_app_id set, slug ending in -pr-42, plus a db.pr_previews row with status='building'; synchronize updates branch on the same preview row; closed marks the preview status='closed' after teardown task runs (~1s); manual DELETE returns {torn_down:true}. (9) Regression — existing push webhook on the demo workspace still returns 202 {status:'queued', deployment_id, branch} via HMAC-signed body; demo workspace 'Acme Studio' confirmed back on agency plan after the run.

minor.endpoint: POST /api/notifications/test (channel=slack | discord with no webhook URL)
minor.issue: Spec says return status:'skipped' (graceful), but endpoint returns {results: []} because services/notifications_sms.py::send_alert at line 88 only loops ('sms','whatsapp','email') when building `available` channels — slack/discord are filtered out BEFORE reaching the dispatcher's slack/discord branch at line 202. Fix: change line 88 to `for ch in ('sms','whatsapp','email','slack','discord'):` so the no-webhook path at L206-213 (which already appends status='skipped') is actually reached.

action_items: (MEDIUM) Fix services/notifications_sms.py::send_alert L88 — add 'slack','discord' to the iteration tuple so the existing skipped-when-no-webhook branch (L206-213) is reachable. This will turn the 2 failing tests GREEN with no test changes needed.

(P3 / cosmetic — carried over from iter9) routers/webhooks.py still emits HTTP 202 for ping/ignored/disabled returns due to default status_code=202 on the route decorator. Spec says 200 for those, 202 only for queued.
critical_code_review_comments: services/notifications_sms.py L88 — the channel allow-list `('sms','whatsapp','email')` should be derived from a single SUPPORTED_CHANNELS constant (or `('sms','whatsapp','email','slack','discord')` directly). Hardcoded subset is the root cause of the slack/discord 'skipped' miss. Duplication risk: routers/notifications.py L70 declares the same set as supported_channels=['sms','whatsapp','email','slack','discord'] — single source of truth would prevent drift.

services/audit.py::log L72 uses asyncio.create_task without holding a reference — fine for a fire-and-forget logger but Python 3.11 GC can drop the task in rare cases. Consider keeping a module-level set of pending tasks and discarding on done_callback (Python docs explicitly warn about this).

services/audit.py L69 `request.headers.get('user-agent')[:200]` will crash with TypeError if the header is missing because the ternary checks the OUTER condition (`request and request.headers.get('user-agent')`) so it's actually safe — confirmed. Just noting for the next reader.

routers/cron.py L31 — `_CRON_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*(\S+\s+){4}\S+\s*$')` matches any non-whitespace tokens but does NOT validate the actual cron field ranges (0-59, 0-23, etc.). A schedule like 'foo bar baz qux quux' passes. Consider using `croniter` (already a common dep) for real validation. Low priority — Coolify will reject garbage downstream.

routers/databases.py::create_database L98 calls `coolify.create_database` synchronously inline — for a real Coolify this is a multi-second blocking call inside the request. Consider moving to BackgroundTasks with status='provisioning' returned immediately (which is the current shape when Coolify is offline). With Coolify reachable, /api/databases POST would currently block on the HTTP call.

routers/databases.py L61 — _redact() is applied on GET but on POST L121 the unredacted record (with connection_string_enc=None) is passed through _redact too. Good. Just confirming connection_string_enc never leaves the API.

services/pr_previews.py L74 `from routers.apps import _redeploy_background` is imported inside the function to avoid circular imports — that's fine, but the same import appears 3 times (L74, L93, L158). Move to a module-level lazy import or stash on the module to avoid 3 separate imports per webhook event.

services/pr_previews.py L100 base_slug uses parent_app['slug'] which is user-controlled and not re-sluggified; if the parent slug contains a digit suffix like '-pr-1' the new slug may collide on a second PR. Recommend running it through services/subdomains.slugify() like the parent app does.

services/pr_previews.py L132 inserts the child app via db.apps.insert_one — but the child has no webhook_secret, webhook_enabled, billing_plan, or workspace_role-mapping fields that the parent flow uses. Result: GET /apps/{preview_id}/webhook would return 404 'no webhook secret'. That's intentional for previews (they don't receive their own webhooks), but worth documenting.
routers/audit.py L43 — pymongo's `find(..., {'_id': 0}).sort().limit().to_list(limit)` correctly applies the limit twice (cursor + to_list arg). Good — no over-fetch.

routers/databases.py — no UPDATE (PUT) endpoint; users can't rename a database or change version after creation. Worth confirming with product that this is intentional (DBs are typically immutable on Coolify).
Backend regression — Demo workspace plan='agency' confirmed via GET /workspaces post-run.

success_rate.backend: 93.5% (29/31). Two failures, both caused by the same one-line bug in send_alert.

test_credentials: demo@deployhub.dev / demo1234 — cookie session via requests.Session; admin@deployhub.dev / admin123 used for platform-wide audit-log calls. Workspace UUID ee9ace3a-0b82-4df5-9dd7-d543c1e0c022 is Acme Studio (agency plan).
seed_data_creation: Per-module fixture creates one TEST_iter10_<hex> app on the demo workspace, used by audit + cron + regression tests; deleted on module teardown. TestDatabases creates one TEST_pg postgres (status='provisioning' because Coolify is unreachable) and deletes it via test_delete_db_and_audit. TestPRPreviews uses its own class-scoped TEST_pr_<hex> parent app and exercises 3 PR numbers (42 opened+sync+closed; 99 manual teardown). All test artefacts cleaned up at end-of-module via fixture finalizers and explicit DELETE calls.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter10 Sprint 5 backend is 93.5% GREEN. The single backend miss is in services/notifications_sms.py L88 — once 'slack' and 'discord' are added to the available-channel iteration tuple, the two failing notification-test cases will pass without any test changes. The new test file /app/backend/tests/test_iter10_sprint5.py is 31 tests across 7 classes, re-runnable as-is. It uses python-dotenv to load REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env (pytest is launched without env so this is required). PR preview tests directly post HMAC-signed pull_request webhooks; cron + databases tests rely on Coolify being unreachable in the preview pod (status='provisioning', coolify_task_uuid=null). Audit log tests use a 300ms sleep after each business mutation because services/audit.py uses asyncio.create_task fire-and-forget. Demo workspace 'Acme Studio' (ee9ace3a-...) is confirmed back on 'agency' plan after the run (TestRegression::test_demo_workspace_on_agency_plan).
rca of the issue: FAILING: TestNotificationPrefs::test_test_send_{slack,discord}_no_url_returns_skipped. Reproduction: PUT /api/notifications/prefs {channels:{}} then POST /api/notifications/test {workspace_id, channel:'slack'} → expected body {results:[{channel:'slack',status:'skipped'}]} but actual is {results:[]}. Root cause: services/notifications_sms.py::send_alert (line 88) builds its `available` list by iterating only ('sms','whatsapp','email'). The endpoint routers/notifications.py::test_send injects the channel into prefs and calls send_alert with channels=[payload.channel], but send_alert filters slack/discord out before reaching the slack/discord dispatcher branch at L202-213 (which already correctly appends status='skipped' when no webhook URL is set). Mitigation: change L88 to iterate over all 5 supported channels — `for ch in ('sms','whatsapp','email','slack','discord'):` — and keep the existing pref-membership check on L89. Single-line fix, no test changes needed.
